title: Israeli Airstrike Kills at Least Five Palestinians Including Three Children in Northern Gaza
detail: On April 22, 2026, an Israeli airstrike in northern Gaza killed at least five Palestinians, including three children, and injured four others. The attack struck Jabalia in the northern Gaza Strip, with additional shelling reported in eastern Gaza City neighborhoods. The incident brought the cumulative number of Palestinians killed by Israeli fire since the October 10, 2025 ceasefire to at least 786, with 2,217 injured — marking a steady pattern of ongoing Israeli operations despite the truce. Palestinian health officials confirmed the casualties. The IDF has not commented specifically on this strike. The Gaza Government Media Office has documented over 2,400 Israeli ceasefire violations since October 2025, including bombardments and shooting incidents along the Yellow Line. The killings occurred amid the ongoing Phase Two negotiation deadlock, in which Hamas has refused to disarm while Israel continues targeted operations against what it calls active threats. The continuing civilian toll has been cited by Hamas as evidence that Israel has not fulfilled its Phase One obligations.

media.caption: Gaza under fire: Israeli strikes continue despite ceasefire — April 22, 2026

title: Hamas Formally Demands Israel Implement Phase One Ceasefire Obligations as Condition for Phase Two Talks
detail: On April 22, 2026, Hamas formally demanded that Israel fully implement all Phase One obligations of the Gaza ceasefire deal before any discussion of Phase Two disarmament could proceed. Hamas's position, articulated through official channels and reported by People's Dispatch, stated that Israel had failed to honor key Phase One commitments including: full IDF withdrawal from populated areas, unrestricted humanitarian access, and release of all Palestinian prisoners agreed under Phase One. Hamas spokesperson Osama Hamdan reiterated that linking disarmament to Phase Two while bypassing Phase One obligations violates the ceasefire framework and contradicts the terms Israel itself agreed to under the October 10, 2025 deal brokered by Qatar, Egypt, and the US. The formal demand came as the second Board of Peace disarmament deadline (April 19) had already passed without Hamas acceptance, and Cairo backchannel talks continued without breakthrough. The US and the Board of Peace maintained that Phase Two — a permanent ceasefire and Palestinian political future — would not begin until disarmament was formally underway. Hamas's Phase One implementation demands are seen as the latest obstacle in what analysts describe as a deeply structurally deadlocked negotiation.

media.caption: Hamas demands Israel implement Phase One of Gaza ceasefire — People's Dispatch, April 22, 2026
